paper_title: Temporally Coherent General Dynamic Scene Reconstruction
paper_claims: An unsupervised multi-view framework reconstructs temporally coherent 4D models of general dynamic scenes captured by static or moving wide-baseline cameras without prior scene or background knowledge by automatically initializing coarse object segmentation and geometry from sparse 3D features, propagating sparse-to-dense temporal correspondences to detect and track dynamic regions, and jointly refining per-view depth and multi-view segmentation under a geodesic star-convexity shape constraint to obtain surfaces with consistent labels and correspondence across time.

source_questions.claim: An unsupervised multi-view framework reconstructs temporally coherent 4D models of general dynamic scenes captured by static or moving wide-baseline cameras without prior scene or background knowledge by automatically initializing coarse object segmentation and geometry from sparse 3D features, propagating sparse-to-dense temporal correspondences to detect and track dynamic regions, and jointly refining per-view depth and multi-view segmentation under a geodesic star-convexity shape constraint to obtain surfaces with consistent labels and correspondence across time.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement accurately and completely describes a framework that reconstructs temporally coherent 4D models of general dynamic scenes? [A] An unsupervised multi-view framework uses static or moving wide-baseline cameras without prior scene or background knowledge, automatically initializes coarse object segmentation and geometry from sparse 3D features, propagates sparse-to-dense temporal correspondences to detect and track dynamic regions, and jointly refines per-view depth and multi-view segmentation under a geodesic star-convexity shape constraint to yield surfaces with consistent labels and correspondences across time. [B] A supervised multi-view framework relies on pre-labeled backgrounds, assumes narrow-baseline static cameras, initializes from dense optical flow, and performs independent per-view depth optimization without shape constraints, producing per-frame surfaces without temporal correspondences. [C] An unsupervised single-view framework requires a prior scene model, tracks motion by keyframe selection only, and refines segmentation alone without imposing any shape constraint, yielding temporally incoherent reconstructions. [D] A semi-supervised multi-view pipeline reconstructs only static scenes by aggregating per-frame meshes from stereo pairs and post-hoc label smoothing, assuming background subtraction is given.

source_questions.claim: An unsupervised multi-view framework reconstructs temporally coherent 4D models of general dynamic scenes captured by static or moving wide-baseline cameras without prior scene or background knowledge by automatically initializing coarse object segmentation and geometry from sparse 3D features, propagating sparse-to-dense temporal correspondences to detect and track dynamic regions, and jointly refining per-view depth and multi-view segmentation under a geodesic star-convexity shape constraint to obtain surfaces with consistent labels and correspondence across time.

paper_title: Refractive Two-View Reconstruction for Underwater 3D Vision
paper_claims: Refraction at a flat interface renders the single-viewpoint approximation invalid and induces depth-dependent geometric bias in underwater imaging when using pinhole-plus-distortion models.

In the case of a pinhole camera observing through a known thin, flat refractive interface, the two-view geometry admits a refractive fundamental matrix that depends on the interface’s position and orientation and the relative camera motion.

Using the refractive fundamental constraint together with refractive reprojection constraints in a two-step estimation improves outlier rejection and yields more accurate and robust underwater relative pose and quasi-dense 3D reconstruction than standard two-view Structure-from-Motion.

paper_title: Disentangling Geometry and Appearance with Regularised Geometry-Aware Generative Adversarial Networks
paper_claims: Conditioning a generative adversarial network on a statistical shape model and enforcing the prior by differentiably warping outputs to a canonical frame disentangles shape from appearance, provides explicit control over object geometry while maintaining realistic texture, constrains the search space to enable effective training on small datasets, and serves as a modular augmentation applicable to diverse GAN architectures.

Training the discriminator with (α,β) regularization to accept slight shape perturbations and reject large ones under the warping transform mitigates label and covariate shift and produces reliable image–shape alignment scores that enable detection of facial landmark tracking or detection errors.

source_questions.claim: Conditioning a generative adversarial network on a statistical shape model and enforcing the prior by differentiably warping outputs to a canonical frame disentangles shape from appearance, provides explicit control over object geometry while maintaining realistic texture, constrains the search space to enable effective training on small datasets, and serves as a modular augmentation applicable to diverse GAN architectures.

[C] It only improves texture realism without affecting geometry control, does not constrain the search space, and cannot be used across different GAN architectures. [D] It eliminates the need for a shape prior or warping, yet still provides geometry control and generalizes only with extensive data.

paper_title: Robust optimization and uncertainty quantification in the nonlinear mechanics of an elevator brake system
paper_claims: In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients and loads, and in realistic operating ranges it is weakly sensitive to cam angle while varying approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, making spring force uncertainty the dominant contributor to braking force variability.

A robust optimization that maximizes a convex combination of worst-case, best-case, mean and inverse standard deviation of braking force under a probabilistic minimum-force constraint produces geometric designs that are less sensitive to parameter uncertainties than those obtained by deterministic optimization.

source_questions.claim: In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients and loads, and in realistic operating ranges it is weakly sensitive to cam angle while varying approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, making spring force uncertainty the dominant contributor to braking force variability.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, which statement correctly characterizes how braking force depends on system parameters and varies across realistic operating ranges? [A] The braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients, and loads; in realistic ranges it is weakly sensitive to cam angle, varies approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, and spring force uncertainty dominates braking force variability. [B] The braking force is an implicit function determined numerically; in realistic ranges it is highly sensitive to cam angle, varies nonlinearly with the spring reaction force, and cam angle uncertainty dominates variability. [C] The braking force depends only on loads; it is insensitive to cam angle and independent of the spring reaction force, so friction coefficient uncertainty dominates variability. [D] The braking force is an explicit function of geometry and friction coefficients only; in realistic ranges it is strongly sensitive to cam angle and varies quadratically with the spring reaction force, so geometric tolerances dominate variability.

source_questions.claim: In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients and loads, and in realistic operating ranges it is weakly sensitive to cam angle while varying approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, making spring force uncertainty the dominant contributor to braking force variability.

[A] Explicit dependence on geometry, friction coefficients, and loads; weak sensitivity to cam angle; approximately affine variation with spring reaction force; and dominance of spring force uncertainty in braking force variability. [B] Explicit dependence on geometry and loads only; strong sensitivity to cam angle; approximately affine variation with spring reaction force; and dominance of cam angle uncertainty in variability. [C] Implicit dependence on geometry, friction coefficients, and loads; weak sensitivity to cam angle; nonlinear dependence on spring reaction force; and dominance of friction coefficient uncertainty in variability. [D] Explicit dependence on geometry, friction coefficients, and loads; strong sensitivity to cam angle; nonlinear dependence on spring reaction force; and dominance of geometric uncertainty in variability.

source_questions.claim: In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients and loads, and in realistic operating ranges it is weakly sensitive to cam angle while varying approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, making spring force uncertainty the dominant contributor to braking force variability.
source_questions.question: Assertion (A): In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients, and loads and, over realistic operating ranges, is weakly sensitive to cam angle while varying approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, making spring force uncertainty the dominant contributor to braking force variability. paper_title: Disk Cluster Allocation Behavior in Windows and NTFS
paper_claims: NTFS on Windows 7 and Windows 10 generally follows a best-fit free-space allocation strategy for block-written files but exhibits systematic deviations where allocations are taken from large areas despite smaller better-fitting holes and fragments are placed leaving small residual gaps.

On fragmented volumes, block-written file fragments decrease in size both within each file and across successive files, whereas stream-written files begin with very small fragments and progressively increase fragment sizes as writing proceeds.

paper_title: A new design for cubic spatially processed wideband arrays
paper_claims: A cubic volumetric array with a single real multiplier per element can steer a fully spatial beam throughout full azimuth and elevation hemispheres while maintaining frequency-invariant beampatterns over a wide fractional bandwidth.

Real-valued element weights are determined by representing the array response as a sum of L symmetric steering terms centered at selected focal points in a bounded three-dimensional frequency space and solving a reduced L-by-L linear system, where even L and symmetric point placement ensure real weights and unit gain in the desired direction.

The design requires solving a linear system of dimension L that can be chosen much smaller than the total number of array elements, thereby substantially reducing computational complexity relative to direct weight computation.

With inter-element spacings not exceeding half the minimum wavelength, the transformed frequency variables are confined to a cube (−0.5, 0.5) in each dimension, supporting frequency-invariant array responses within the operational band.
Increasing the number of symmetric focal points narrows the main lobe and suppresses sidelobes without compromising steering capability or frequency invariance.

[D] A cubic volumetric array using per-element time delays that produces beampatterns varying with frequency over a narrow bandwidth.

paper_title: Local dimension reduction of summary statistics for likelihood-free inference
paper_claims: A localization strategy for projection-based dimension reduction in likelihood-free inference, where transformations are fitted in a neighborhood of the observed data and localization and transformation parameters are optimized using validation datasets, yields informative low-dimensional summaries that improve posterior estimation accuracy and stability and mitigate failure of transformed summaries outside the local region across diverse models.

paper_title: Robust Bayesian model selection for variable clustering with the Gaussian graphical model
paper_claims: The maximum a posteriori estimation for this model admits a strictly convex reformulation in the precision parameters that is solvable to global optimality using a three-block ADMM algorithm with convergence guaranteed for any positive penalty parameter.

Classical BIC and Laplace-type approximations are inappropriate for this model because the maximum-likelihood fit and parameter count do not depend on the clustering, yielding identical scores across clusterings.

Restricting the hypothesis space to candidate clusterings produced by spectral clustering on Graphical Lasso precision estimates yields a tractable set that often contains the true or near-true clustering, enabling practical Bayesian model selection.

paper_title: Person Re-identification in Identity Regression Space
paper_claims: Formulating person re-identification as regression into an identity regression space where each training identity is assigned an orthogonal unit target vector yields an embedding that collapses intra-class variance and maximizes inter-class separateness.

The regression-based re-identification model supports online incremental learning by updating the projection with Sherman–Morrison–Woodbury identities using only newly labelled samples, eliminating retraining and past-data storage.

Active learning that jointly exploits exploration of appearance diversity and unexplained cross-view matching discrepancy prioritizes informative samples for annotation, improving label efficiency during incremental updates.

Encoding class targets as orthogonal one-hot vectors strengthens global between-class discrimination while retaining extreme intra-class compactness compared to null-space Fisher and FDA-style embeddings.

paper_title: A diachronic perspective on peer disagreement in veritistic social epistemology
paper_claims: In a Bayesian, veritistic evaluation of peer disagreement that updates both credences and inter-agent trust, the conciliatory response of lowering confidence and trust upon unexpected disagreement increases expected truth-approximation only when agents continue to recheck the target proposition over time, whereas without such ongoing inquiry it confers no veritistic advantage over steadfastness.

[A] It increases expected truth-approximation only if agents continue to recheck the target proposition over time; without ongoing inquiry it offers no advantage over steadfastness. [B] It increases expected truth-approximation irrespective of whether agents recheck, and thus strictly dominates steadfastness. [C] It never increases expected truth-approximation, even with continued rechecking over time. [D] It increases expected truth-approximation only when agents halt further inquiry.

paper_title: Face Mask Extraction in Video Sequence
paper_claims: A ConvLSTM-augmented fully convolutional network that processes video sequences learns temporal dependencies alongside spatial features to perform end-to-end dense segmentation of facial components in video.

A segmentation loss derived from the intersection-over-union objective that assigns separate per-class weights to positive and negative samples directly optimizes mean IoU and mitigates class imbalance more effectively than cross-entropy and naive IoU losses.
A coarse-to-fine pipeline that combines a primary whole-face model with zoomed-in eye and mouth models yields more accurate component masks than a single-scale model alone.

[B] It processes single images, ignores temporal information, and performs two-stage sparse keypoint detection of generic objects. [C] It processes video sequences but learns only spatial features and outputs image-level emotion classification. paper_title: Reducing false wake-up in contention-based wake-up control of wireless LANs
paper_claims: In wake-up radio assisted contention-based transmitter wake-up for WLANs, the WLAN module’s wake-up latency induces multiple false activations that dominate energy overhead relative to collisions, and jointly applying backoff counter resetting to emulate freezing during wake-up, early sleep upon inferred false activation, and contention window tuning reduces false-wake probability and duration while improving the throughput–energy tradeoff.

False wake-up probability increases with the WLAN module wake-up latency, and for sufficiently large contention windows the ratio of false wake-ups to collisions approaches the wake-up period expressed in slots.

Selecting the contention window by maximizing the product of spectral energy efficiency and channel efficiency, using wake-up radio observations of idle intervals to inform contention levels, yields higher overall efficiency than configurations based solely on throughput or collision energy.

[B] Wake-up latency has negligible impact on activation patterns, collisions dominate energy overhead, and enabling RTS/CTS with larger payloads eliminates false wakes but degrades the throughput–energy tradeoff. [C] Wake-up latency decreases the likelihood of false activations, collisions chiefly determine energy overhead, and applying only contention window tuning lowers false-wake duration but worsens the throughput–energy tradeoff. [D] Wake-up latency causes multiple false activations, yet energy overhead is dominated by collisions, and using transmit power control and higher carrier sensing thresholds reduces collisions but degrades the throughput–energy tradeoff.

paper_title: Probabilistic Approach Versus Machine Learning for One-Shot Quad-Tree Prediction in an Intra HEVC Encoder
paper_claims: For HEVC intra coding, per-depth thresholds on CU luminance variance derived from a learning frame and modeled as a linear function of the mean and standard deviation of the variance distribution enable one-shot prediction of CTU quad-tree partitioning largely independent of quantization parameter.

Lightweight decision-tree classifiers using local variance-based features of CUs, sub-CUs, neighboring CUs, and QP accurately predict per-depth split/merge decisions, enabling one-shot quad-tree partitioning without exhaustive RDO.

Constraining rate–distortion optimization to the interval between an initial variance-threshold-based depth map and a refined depth map drastically reduces quad-tree search complexity while preserving compression performance within a controlled trade-off.

[C] Per-CTU thresholds on CU luminance variance, learned without a learning frame and fixed across all depths, modeled as constants. paper_title: QEX: target-specific druglikeness filter enhances ligand-based virtual screening
paper_claims: A target-specific druglikeness index constructed by modeling the distributions of eight physicochemical properties from known actives and aggregating them via weighted desirability functions provides superior enrichment of actives for individual targets compared with a global druglikeness metric trained on approved drugs.

By relying solely on active compounds and capturing binding-pocket physicochemical preferences, a target-specific druglikeness index enables effective screening when inactive data are unavailable and complements oral-drug-focused indices that emphasize ADMET-related constraints.

paper_title: An adaptive LTE listen-before-talk scheme towards a fair coexistence with Wi-Fi in unlicensed spectrum
paper_claims: Fixed LTE Licensed-Assisted Access channel occupancy durations that are much longer than single-packet Wi‑Fi transmissions, particularly when Wi‑Fi does not aggregate frames, create imbalanced channel occupancy that can yield unfair coexistence in co-channel deployments.

An adaptive LTE‑U listen‑before‑talk mechanism that performs clear channel assessment before each burst and then uses a tunable transmission opportunity followed by a tunable muting period, with subframe‑aligned reservation signaling, enables fair, technology‑agnostic coexistence with Wi‑Fi and other unlicensed systems by balancing access opportunities according to network density and traffic requirements while preserving time‑critical LTE signaling on licensed spectrum.

Fair coexistence in unlicensed bands is more appropriately defined as equal sharing of wireless resources among co‑located networks rather than among different radio technologies, with throughput and channel occupancy serving as practical fairness indicators.

source_questions.claim: Fixed LTE Licensed-Assisted Access channel occupancy durations that are much longer than single-packet Wi‑Fi transmissions, particularly when Wi‑Fi does not aggregate frames, create imbalanced channel occupancy that can yield unfair coexistence in co-channel deployments.

[A] LAA uses fixed, long channel occupancy bursts relative to single-packet, non-aggregated Wi‑Fi, leading to disproportionate airtime and potential unfair coexistence. [B] LAA uses very short channel occupancy bursts comparable to Wi‑Fi aggregated transmissions, producing balanced airtime and fair coexistence. [C] LAA and Wi‑Fi operate on different channels, eliminating coexistence concerns regardless of occupancy durations. [D] Wi‑Fi aggregates frames while LAA varies its occupancy adaptively, ensuring fairness independent of relative burst lengths.

paper_title: Providing Reliable Communications over Static-node-assisted Vehicular Networks Using Distance-vector Routing
paper_claims: RDV is a table-driven distance-vector routing protocol for static-node-assisted vehicular delay-tolerant networks that, using statistic-based vehicle selection to estimate local movement probabilities and adaptive packet duplication to satisfy a preconfigured expected end-to-end delivery ratio, computes shortest paths with a traffic-aware metric through lightweight neighbor exchanges without roadmaps, thereby enabling reliable communication under sparse and unreliable static-node deployment with low control overhead.

source_questions.claim: RDV is a table-driven distance-vector routing protocol for static-node-assisted vehicular delay-tolerant networks that, using statistic-based vehicle selection to estimate local movement probabilities and adaptive packet duplication to satisfy a preconfigured expected end-to-end delivery ratio, computes shortest paths with a traffic-aware metric through lightweight neighbor exchanges without roadmaps, thereby enabling reliable communication under sparse and unreliable static-node deployment with low control overhead.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which option fully characterizes RDV in terms of routing style, target network, mechanisms, path computation, information requirements, and resulting properties? [A] A table-driven distance-vector protocol for static-node-assisted vehicular delay-tolerant networks that estimates local movement probabilities via statistic-based vehicle selection, adaptively duplicates packets to meet a preconfigured expected end-to-end delivery ratio, computes shortest paths using a traffic-aware metric through lightweight neighbor exchanges without roadmaps, and thereby provides reliable communication under sparse and unreliable static-node deployment with low control overhead. [B] A reactive link-state protocol for general mobile ad hoc networks that relies on preloaded roadmaps and global traffic maps, avoids packet duplication to minimize bandwidth, and emphasizes maximum-throughput routes even when static nodes are dense, with high control overhead. [C] A geographic greedy protocol for vehicular networks without static nodes, which selects next hops solely by instantaneous GPS positions, floods packets to maximize delivery, computes minimal-hop paths without traffic awareness, and requires detailed roadmaps. [D] A table-driven distance-vector protocol for static-node-assisted vehicular DTNs that uses fixed-k replication without movement estimation, computes shortest paths by hop count with periodic full-table exchanges and city roadmaps, achieving reliability only when static nodes are dense with moderate overhead.

[D] A delay-tolerant routing heuristic for urban sensing that adopts opportunistic store-carry-forward without neighbor exchanges, relies exclusively on static nodes, and deliberately avoids any shortest-path computation or delivery-ratio targets.

paper_title: Estimation of and correction for finite motion sampling errors in small animal PET rigid motion correction

paper_title: Discovering the Hidden Community Structure of Public Transportation Networks
paper_claims: A transfer network that represents atomic passenger groups as maximal cliques linked by directed transfer edges, together with a community network that connects passenger pairs using a connection-strength metric that rewards shared transfers across different vehicle trips and penalizes repeated co-presence within the same trip, captures similarity in rider travel behavior beyond contact duration alone.

In an activity-based simulation of the Twin Cities public transit system, frequent multi-trip travel corridors primarily link outlying suburbs to the city center during peak hours and epidemic spreading risk concentrates on routes that cross or connect to the city center.

source_questions.claim: A transfer network that represents atomic passenger groups as maximal cliques linked by directed transfer edges, together with a community network that connects passenger pairs using a connection-strength metric that rewards shared transfers across different vehicle trips and penalizes repeated co-presence within the same trip, captures similarity in rider travel behavior beyond contact duration alone.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): An approach combines a transfer network that represents atomic passenger groups as maximal cliques linked by directed transfer edges with a community network that connects passenger pairs using a connection-strength metric that rewards shared transfers across different vehicle trips and penalizes repeated co-presence within the same trip to capture similarity in rider travel behavior beyond contact duration alone. Which option matches this description? [A] The transfer network models atomic groups as maximal cliques with directed transfer edges, the community network connects pairs by a metric that rewards cross-trip shared transfers and penalizes repeated same-trip co-presence, and together they capture rider-behavior similarity beyond contact duration alone. [B] The transfer network models atomic groups as chains with undirected edges, the community network uses contact duration only, and the combined model does not improve upon contact duration. [C] The transfer network uses maximal cliques but undirected transfer edges, the community network rewards repeated co-presence within the same trip and ignores transfers across different trips, and the approach relies solely on contact duration. [D] The transfer network uses random subgraphs with directed edges, the community network links any pair sharing a trip without weighting, and the result captures only contact duration.

paper_title: In vivo estimation of passive biomechanical properties of human myocardium
paper_claims: Maintaining fixed ratios among the anisotropic parameters (af:as:afs and bf:bs:bfs) and estimating only four scalars (a, b, Ka, Kb) preserves the experimentally observed shear stiffness ordering and suffices to calibrate the model from limited data.

Identified passive myocardial parameters depend more strongly on the assumed left-ventricular end-diastolic pressure than on ventricular geometry or fibre helix angle, with the stiffness nonlinearity parameter b being most sensitive.
In vivo human myocardial passive properties are less stiff than pig-derived or ex vivo human measurements, which otherwise yield unrealistically stiff diastolic behavior in human ventricular models.

Across subjects and within physiological variations of pressure and fibre orientation, the predicted shear stress–strain behavior in dominant shear modes remains similar when parameters are constrained to the estimated ranges.

paper_title: A Novel Gaussian–Bernoulli Based Convolutional Deep Belief Networks for Image Feature Extraction

paper_title: Predicting user demographics from music listening information
paper_claims: Age, gender, and nationality can be inferred from music listening histories by encoding listened artists and artist tags with TF-IDF and PCA and augmenting with behavioral scores, yielding supervised models that outperform majority baselines and remain effective with extremely sparse listening data, and the inferred attributes can be used to improve music recommendations.

[A] Listened artists and artist tags are encoded with TF-IDF and PCA and augmented with behavioral scores to train supervised models that outperform majority baselines, remain effective with extremely sparse listening data, and whose inferred attributes improve music recommendations. [B] Listened artists and artist tags are encoded only with TF-IDF without PCA and no behavioral scores, used in unsupervised models that match majority baselines, require dense listening data, and do not improve recommendations. [C] Only age is inferred using PCA on user behavior scores without artist tags, yielding supervised models that do not beat majority baselines, are ineffective with sparse data, and have no impact on recommendations. [D] Artists and artist tags are encoded with TF-IDF and PCA and augmented with behavioral scores to train supervised models that underperform majority baselines, fail with sparse data, and cannot be used to improve recommendations.

paper_title: Approximation of quantum control correction scheme using deep neural networks
paper_claims: Bidirectional long short-term memory networks approximate the functional mapping from normal control pulses for an ideal two-qubit system to de-noising pulses that compensate coherent drift, enabling generation of effective controls without explicit system modelling.

The learnability and accuracy of the NCP-to-DCP mapping depend on the drift structure, being easier when the drift Hamiltonian resembles the base spin-chain interaction and harder for asymmetric single-qubit drifts orthogonal to the available controls.

The learned mapping exhibits continuity and approximate symmetry with respect to local perturbations of the input pulses, as small changes in control amplitudes at nearby times or along different control axes induce statistically similar variations in the corrected pulses over mid evolution windows.
For weak Markovian incoherent noise modeled by Lindblad operators, learning-based correction of control pulses does not provide a meaningful performance gain over uncorrected pulses.

Optimizing for robustness by training on Gaussian-amplitude–perturbed copies of the generated pulses fails to produce controls with improved fluctuation resilience, indicating limitations of gradient-based learning for this robustness objective.

paper_title: Automated segmentation of trabecular and cortical bone from proton density weighted MRI of the knee
paper_claims: Combining a 3D local intensity clustering-based level set that jointly estimates MRI bias fields with normal-vector intensity line profiling and variance-minimizing, thickness-constrained optimization enables automatic delineation of trabecular and cortical bone of the femur and tibia in proton density weighted knee MRI without training data, robust to intensity inhomogeneity and weak edges near ligaments.
